Final Code Customer Retention

Annika Rodriguez

2026-04-23

ANNIKA

# Libraries
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.1.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(caret)
## Loading required package: lattice
## 
## Attaching package: 'caret'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     lift
# Load dataset

df <- read.csv("clean_customerretention_dataset.csv")

df$acquisition <- as.factor(df$acquisition)
df$industry    <- as.factor(df$industry)

# Check structure
str(df)
## 'data.frame':    500 obs. of  14 variables:
##  $ acquisition: Factor w/ 2 levels "0","1": 2 2 2 1 2 2 2 2 1 1 ...
##  $ duration   : int  1635 1039 1288 0 1631 942 799 1081 0 0 ...
##  $ profit     : num  6134 3524 4081 -638 5446 ...
##  $ acq_exp    : num  694 460 249 638 589 ...
##  $ ret_exp    : num  972 450 805 0 920 ...
##  $ acq_exp_sq : num  480998 211628 62016 407644 346897 ...
##  $ ret_exp_sq : num  943929 202077 648089 0 846106 ...
##  $ freq       : int  6 11 21 0 2 7 15 13 0 0 ...
##  $ freq_sq    : int  36 121 441 0 4 49 225 169 0 0 ...
##  $ crossbuy   : int  5 6 6 0 9 4 5 5 0 0 ...
##  $ sow        : int  95 22 90 0 80 48 51 23 0 0 ...
##  $ industry   : Factor w/ 2 levels "0","1": 2 1 1 1 1 2 1 2 1 2 ...
##  $ revenue    : num  47.2 45.1 29.1 40.6 48.7 ...
##  $ employees  : int  898 686 1423 181 631 617 911 645 530 511 ...
summary(df)
##  acquisition    duration          profit           acq_exp       
##  0:162       Min.   :   0.0   Min.   :-1027.0   Min.   :   1.21  
##  1:338       1st Qu.:   0.0   1st Qu.: -316.3   1st Qu.: 384.14  
##              Median : 957.5   Median : 3369.9   Median : 491.66  
##              Mean   : 742.5   Mean   : 2403.8   Mean   : 493.35  
##              3rd Qu.:1146.2   3rd Qu.: 3931.6   3rd Qu.: 600.21  
##              Max.   :1673.0   Max.   : 6134.3   Max.   :1027.04  
##     ret_exp         acq_exp_sq          ret_exp_sq           freq      
##  Min.   :   0.0   Min.   :1.460e+00   Min.   :      0   Min.   : 0.00  
##  1st Qu.:   0.0   1st Qu.:1.476e+05   1st Qu.:      0   1st Qu.: 0.00  
##  Median : 398.1   Median :2.417e+05   Median : 158480   Median : 6.00  
##  Mean   : 336.3   Mean   :2.712e+05   Mean   : 184000   Mean   : 6.22  
##  3rd Qu.: 514.3   3rd Qu.:3.602e+05   3rd Qu.: 264466   3rd Qu.:11.00  
##  Max.   :1095.0   Max.   :1.055e+06   Max.   :1198937   Max.   :21.00  
##     freq_sq          crossbuy           sow         industry    revenue     
##  Min.   :  0.00   Min.   : 0.000   Min.   :  0.00   0:239    Min.   :14.49  
##  1st Qu.:  0.00   1st Qu.: 0.000   1st Qu.:  0.00   1:261    1st Qu.:33.53  
##  Median : 36.00   Median : 5.000   Median : 44.00            Median :41.43  
##  Mean   : 69.25   Mean   : 4.052   Mean   : 38.88            Mean   :40.54  
##  3rd Qu.:121.00   3rd Qu.: 7.000   3rd Qu.: 66.00            3rd Qu.:47.52  
##  Max.   :441.00   Max.   :11.000   Max.   :116.00            Max.   :65.10  
##    employees     
##  Min.   :  18.0  
##  1st Qu.: 503.0  
##  Median : 657.5  
##  Mean   : 671.5  
##  3rd Qu.: 826.0  
##  Max.   :1461.0
#Identify Target Variable
table(df$acquisition)
## 
##   0   1 
## 162 338
prop.table(table(df$acquisition))
## 
##     0     1 
## 0.324 0.676
colnames(df)
##  [1] "acquisition" "duration"    "profit"      "acq_exp"     "ret_exp"    
##  [6] "acq_exp_sq"  "ret_exp_sq"  "freq"        "freq_sq"     "crossbuy"   
## [11] "sow"         "industry"    "revenue"     "employees"
head(df)
##   acquisition duration  profit acq_exp ret_exp acq_exp_sq ret_exp_sq freq
## 1           1     1635 6134.30  693.54  971.56  480997.73   943928.8    6
## 2           1     1039 3523.62  460.03  449.53  211627.60   202077.2   11
## 3           1     1288 4080.62  249.03  805.04   62015.94   648089.4   21
## 4           0        0 -638.47  638.47    0.00  407643.94        0.0    0
## 5           1     1631 5446.32  588.98  919.84  346897.44   846105.6    2
## 6           1      942 3488.07  568.65  365.11  323362.82   133305.3    7
##   freq_sq crossbuy sow industry revenue employees
## 1      36        5  95        1   47.20       898
## 2     121        6  22        0   45.11       686
## 3     441        6  90        0   29.10      1423
## 4       0        0   0        0   40.64       181
## 5       4        9  80        0   48.72       631
## 6      49        4  48        1   35.43       617
#train/test split

set.seed(123)

train_index <- createDataPartition(df$acquisition, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)

train_data <- df[train_index, ]
test_data  <- df[-train_index, ]
#EDA visial ex 1

ggplot(df, aes(x = acquisition)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "lightpink") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Customer Acquisition",
       x = "Acquisition (0 = No, 1 = Yes)",
       y = "Number of Customers") +
  theme_minimal()

#EDA Visial ex 2

ggplot(df, aes(x = acquisition, y = profit)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(title = "Profit by Acquisition Outcome",
       x = "Acquisition",
       y = "Profit") +
  theme_minimal()

#logistic regression

log_model <- glm(acquisition ~ ., data = train_data, family = "binomial")
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
summary(log_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = acquisition ~ ., family = "binomial", data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept) -2.909e+01  1.847e+05       0        1
## duration     1.741e-01  1.236e+03       0        1
## profit       2.579e-03  7.033e+01       0        1
## acq_exp      7.412e-03  4.911e+02       0        1
## ret_exp     -3.853e-01  3.481e+03       0        1
## acq_exp_sq  -9.703e-06  6.061e-01       0        1
## ret_exp_sq   1.271e-04  1.539e+00       0        1
## freq         3.970e-01  1.855e+04       0        1
## freq_sq      7.138e-02  1.507e+03       0        1
## crossbuy     5.085e-01  1.566e+04       0        1
## sow         -7.423e-03  9.743e+02       0        1
## industry1    5.045e-01  4.679e+04       0        1
## revenue      1.766e-02  2.343e+03       0        1
## employees    3.676e-03  9.722e+01       0        1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 5.0525e+02  on 400  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 3.4979e-09  on 387  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 28
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 25
#predictions and evals

log_probs <- predict(log_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

log_pred <- ifelse(log_probs > 0.5, 1, 0)
log_pred <- as.factor(log_pred)

log_pred <- factor(log_pred, levels = levels(test_data$acquisition))

confusionMatrix(log_pred, test_data$acquisition)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 32  0
##          1  0 67
##                                      
##                Accuracy : 1          
##                  95% CI : (0.9634, 1)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768     
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : < 2.2e-16  
##                                      
##                   Kappa : 1          
##                                      
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : NA         
##                                      
##             Sensitivity : 1.0000     
##             Specificity : 1.0000     
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.0000     
##          Neg Pred Value : 1.0000     
##              Prevalence : 0.3232     
##          Detection Rate : 0.3232     
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3232     
##       Balanced Accuracy : 1.0000     
##                                      
##        'Positive' Class : 0          
## 
#removing squared terms to reduce redundancy
log_mods <- glm(acquisition ~ . - acq_exp_sq - ret_exp_sq - freq_sq, data = train_data, family = "binomial")
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
summary(log_mods)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = acquisition ~ . - acq_exp_sq - ret_exp_sq - freq_sq, 
##     family = "binomial", data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept) -2.517e+01  1.364e+05       0        1
## duration     7.886e-02  2.657e+02       0        1
## profit       2.591e-03  5.959e+01       0        1
## acq_exp     -1.376e-03  1.031e+02       0        1
## ret_exp     -1.039e-01  2.601e+02       0        1
## freq         9.726e-01  5.150e+03       0        1
## crossbuy     7.205e-01  1.514e+04       0        1
## sow          3.602e-02  9.977e+02       0        1
## industry1   -8.692e-01  4.598e+04       0        1
## revenue     -5.139e-03  2.444e+03       0        1
## employees    1.985e-03  9.438e+01       0        1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 5.0525e+02  on 400  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 3.7473e-09  on 390  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 22
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 25
#predictions and evals

log_prob <- predict(log_mods, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

log_preds <- ifelse(log_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)
log_preds <- as.factor(log_preds)

log_preds <- factor(log_preds, levels = levels(test_data$acquisition))

confusionMatrix(log_preds, test_data$acquisition)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 32  0
##          1  0 67
##                                      
##                Accuracy : 1          
##                  95% CI : (0.9634, 1)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768     
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : < 2.2e-16  
##                                      
##                   Kappa : 1          
##                                      
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : NA         
##                                      
##             Sensitivity : 1.0000     
##             Specificity : 1.0000     
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.0000     
##          Neg Pred Value : 1.0000     
##              Prevalence : 0.3232     
##          Detection Rate : 0.3232     
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3232     
##       Balanced Accuracy : 1.0000     
##                                      
##        'Positive' Class : 0          
##

YASMINE

# Libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
## Warning: package 'rpart.plot' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(corrplot)
## corrplot 0.95 loaded
# Load dataset
df <- read.csv("clean_customerretention_dataset.csv")

# Check structure
str(df)
## 'data.frame':    500 obs. of  14 variables:
##  $ acquisition: int  1 1 1 0 1 1 1 1 0 0 ...
##  $ duration   : int  1635 1039 1288 0 1631 942 799 1081 0 0 ...
##  $ profit     : num  6134 3524 4081 -638 5446 ...
##  $ acq_exp    : num  694 460 249 638 589 ...
##  $ ret_exp    : num  972 450 805 0 920 ...
##  $ acq_exp_sq : num  480998 211628 62016 407644 346897 ...
##  $ ret_exp_sq : num  943929 202077 648089 0 846106 ...
##  $ freq       : int  6 11 21 0 2 7 15 13 0 0 ...
##  $ freq_sq    : int  36 121 441 0 4 49 225 169 0 0 ...
##  $ crossbuy   : int  5 6 6 0 9 4 5 5 0 0 ...
##  $ sow        : int  95 22 90 0 80 48 51 23 0 0 ...
##  $ industry   : int  1 0 0 0 0 1 0 1 0 1 ...
##  $ revenue    : num  47.2 45.1 29.1 40.6 48.7 ...
##  $ employees  : int  898 686 1423 181 631 617 911 645 530 511 ...
summary(df)
##   acquisition       duration          profit           acq_exp       
##  Min.   :0.000   Min.   :   0.0   Min.   :-1027.0   Min.   :   1.21  
##  1st Qu.:0.000   1st Qu.:   0.0   1st Qu.: -316.3   1st Qu.: 384.14  
##  Median :1.000   Median : 957.5   Median : 3369.9   Median : 491.66  
##  Mean   :0.676   Mean   : 742.5   Mean   : 2403.8   Mean   : 493.35  
##  3rd Qu.:1.000   3rd Qu.:1146.2   3rd Qu.: 3931.6   3rd Qu.: 600.21  
##  Max.   :1.000   Max.   :1673.0   Max.   : 6134.3   Max.   :1027.04  
##     ret_exp         acq_exp_sq          ret_exp_sq           freq      
##  Min.   :   0.0   Min.   :1.460e+00   Min.   :      0   Min.   : 0.00  
##  1st Qu.:   0.0   1st Qu.:1.476e+05   1st Qu.:      0   1st Qu.: 0.00  
##  Median : 398.1   Median :2.417e+05   Median : 158480   Median : 6.00  
##  Mean   : 336.3   Mean   :2.712e+05   Mean   : 184000   Mean   : 6.22  
##  3rd Qu.: 514.3   3rd Qu.:3.602e+05   3rd Qu.: 264466   3rd Qu.:11.00  
##  Max.   :1095.0   Max.   :1.055e+06   Max.   :1198937   Max.   :21.00  
##     freq_sq          crossbuy           sow            industry    
##  Min.   :  0.00   Min.   : 0.000   Min.   :  0.00   Min.   :0.000  
##  1st Qu.:  0.00   1st Qu.: 0.000   1st Qu.:  0.00   1st Qu.:0.000  
##  Median : 36.00   Median : 5.000   Median : 44.00   Median :1.000  
##  Mean   : 69.25   Mean   : 4.052   Mean   : 38.88   Mean   :0.522  
##  3rd Qu.:121.00   3rd Qu.: 7.000   3rd Qu.: 66.00   3rd Qu.:1.000  
##  Max.   :441.00   Max.   :11.000   Max.   :116.00   Max.   :1.000  
##     revenue        employees     
##  Min.   :14.49   Min.   :  18.0  
##  1st Qu.:33.53   1st Qu.: 503.0  
##  Median :41.43   Median : 657.5  
##  Mean   :40.54   Mean   : 671.5  
##  3rd Qu.:47.52   3rd Qu.: 826.0  
##  Max.   :65.10   Max.   :1461.0

#Identify Target Variable

table(df$acquisition)        # or whatever the column is called
## 
##   0   1 
## 162 338
prop.table(table(df$acquisition))
## 
##     0     1 
## 0.324 0.676
colnames(df)
##  [1] "acquisition" "duration"    "profit"      "acq_exp"     "ret_exp"    
##  [6] "acq_exp_sq"  "ret_exp_sq"  "freq"        "freq_sq"     "crossbuy"   
## [11] "sow"         "industry"    "revenue"     "employees"
head(df)
##   acquisition duration  profit acq_exp ret_exp acq_exp_sq ret_exp_sq freq
## 1           1     1635 6134.30  693.54  971.56  480997.73   943928.8    6
## 2           1     1039 3523.62  460.03  449.53  211627.60   202077.2   11
## 3           1     1288 4080.62  249.03  805.04   62015.94   648089.4   21
## 4           0        0 -638.47  638.47    0.00  407643.94        0.0    0
## 5           1     1631 5446.32  588.98  919.84  346897.44   846105.6    2
## 6           1      942 3488.07  568.65  365.11  323362.82   133305.3    7
##   freq_sq crossbuy sow industry revenue employees
## 1      36        5  95        1   47.20       898
## 2     121        6  22        0   45.11       686
## 3     441        6  90        0   29.10      1423
## 4       0        0   0        0   40.64       181
## 5       4        9  80        0   48.72       631
## 6      49        4  48        1   35.43       617

#Step 3: EDA

#1. Target Distribution

ggplot(df, aes(x = factor(acquisition))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(title = "Customer Acquisition Distribution",
       x = "Acquired (0 = No, 1 = Yes)",
       y = "Count")

#2. Numeric Distributions

num_vars <- df %>% select(where(is.numeric))

num_vars |>
  gather() |>
  ggplot(aes(value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "darkgreen") +
  facet_wrap(~key, scales = "free")

#3. Boxplots vs Target

num_vars |>
  mutate(acquired = df$acquisition) |>
  gather(key, value, -acquisition) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(acquisition), y = value, fill = factor(acquisition))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~key, scales = "free") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

#4. Correlation Heatmap

corr_matrix <- cor(num_vars, use = "complete.obs")

corrplot(corr_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper")

#Step 4: Train/Test Split

set.seed(123)

df$acquisition <- as.factor(df$acquisition)

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(df$acquisition, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

train_data <- df[trainIndex, ]
test_data  <- df[-trainIndex, ]

dim(train_data)
## [1] 401  14
dim(test_data)
## [1] 99 14
prop.table(table(train_data$acquisition))
## 
##         0         1 
## 0.3241895 0.6758105
prop.table(table(test_data$acquisition))
## 
##         0         1 
## 0.3232323 0.6767677

#Step 5: Decision Tree

tree_model <- rpart(acquisition ~ ., 
                    data = train_data, 
                    method = "class",
                    control = rpart.control(cp = 0.01))
rpart.plot(tree_model)

pred_tree <- predict(tree_model, test_data, type = "class")
confusionMatrix(pred_tree, test_data$acquisition)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 32  0
##          1  0 67
##                                      
##                Accuracy : 1          
##                  95% CI : (0.9634, 1)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768     
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : < 2.2e-16  
##                                      
##                   Kappa : 1          
##                                      
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : NA         
##                                      
##             Sensitivity : 1.0000     
##             Specificity : 1.0000     
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.0000     
##          Neg Pred Value : 1.0000     
##              Prevalence : 0.3232     
##          Detection Rate : 0.3232     
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3232     
##       Balanced Accuracy : 1.0000     
##                                      
##        'Positive' Class : 0          
##

#How strong is duration?

table(df$acquisition, df$duration == 0)
##    
##     FALSE TRUE
##   0     0  162
##   1   338    0

#Model without duration

tree_model2 <- rpart(acquisition ~ . - duration, 
                     data = train_data, 
                     method = "class")

pred_tree2 <- predict(tree_model2, test_data, type = "class")

confusionMatrix(pred_tree2, test_data$acquisition)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 32  0
##          1  0 67
##                                      
##                Accuracy : 1          
##                  95% CI : (0.9634, 1)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768     
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : < 2.2e-16  
##                                      
##                   Kappa : 1          
##                                      
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : NA         
##                                      
##             Sensitivity : 1.0000     
##             Specificity : 1.0000     
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.0000     
##          Neg Pred Value : 1.0000     
##              Prevalence : 0.3232     
##          Detection Rate : 0.3232     
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3232     
##       Balanced Accuracy : 1.0000     
##                                      
##        'Positive' Class : 0          
##

#Drop leakage variables

df_clean <- df |>
  select(acquisition, acq_exp, acq_exp_sq, profit, industry, revenue, employees)

#Train/Test split

set.seed(123)

df_clean$acquisition <- as.factor(df_clean$acquisition)

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(df_clean$acquisition, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

train_clean <- df_clean[trainIndex, ]
test_clean  <- df_clean[-trainIndex, ]

#Cleaned Decision Tree

tree_clean <- rpart(acquisition ~ ., 
                    data = train_clean, 
                    method = "class")

rpart.plot(tree_clean)

#Evaluate new model

pred_clean <- predict(tree_clean, test_clean, type = "class")

confusionMatrix(pred_clean, test_clean$acquisition)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 32  0
##          1  0 67
##                                      
##                Accuracy : 1          
##                  95% CI : (0.9634, 1)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768     
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : < 2.2e-16  
##                                      
##                   Kappa : 1          
##                                      
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : NA         
##                                      
##             Sensitivity : 1.0000     
##             Specificity : 1.0000     
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.0000     
##          Neg Pred Value : 1.0000     
##              Prevalence : 0.3232     
##          Detection Rate : 0.3232     
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3232     
##       Balanced Accuracy : 1.0000     
##                                      
##        'Positive' Class : 0          
##

#Keep only true “pre-acquisition” variable

df_real <- df |>
  select(acquisition, industry, revenue, employees)

#Re-run model

set.seed(123)

df_real$acquisition <- as.factor(df_real$acquisition)

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(df_real$acquisition, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

train_real <- df_real[trainIndex, ]
test_real  <- df_real[-trainIndex, ]

tree_real <- rpart(acquisition ~ ., data = train_real, method = "class")

rpart.plot(tree_real)

pred_real <- predict(tree_real, test_real, type = "class")

confusionMatrix(pred_real, test_real$acquisition)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 15 10
##          1 17 57
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.7273         
##                  95% CI : (0.6285, 0.812)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768         
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.1670         
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.3389         
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.2482         
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.4688         
##             Specificity : 0.8507         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.6000         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.7703         
##              Prevalence : 0.3232         
##          Detection Rate : 0.1515         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.2525         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.6597         
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : 0              
##

PEYTON

# Person C: Random Forest + Model Comparison 

library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
## randomForest 4.7-1.2
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
df <- read.csv("clean_customerretention_dataset.csv")
df_real <- df |>
  select(acquisition, industry, revenue, employees)
df_real$acquisition <- as.factor(df_real$acquisition)

train/test

set.seed(123)

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(df_real$acquisition, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)

train_real <- df_real[trainIndex, ]
test_real  <- df_real[-trainIndex, ]

dim(train_real)
## [1] 401   4
dim(test_real)
## [1] 99  4
prop.table(table(train_real$acquisition))
## 
##         0         1 
## 0.3241895 0.6758105
prop.table(table(test_real$acquisition))
## 
##         0         1 
## 0.3232323 0.6767677

Log Regression

set.seed(123)

log_model <- train(
  acquisition ~ .,
  data = train_real,
  method = "glm",
  family = "binomial")

log_pred <- predict(log_model, newdata = test_real)

log_cm <- confusionMatrix(log_pred, test_real$acquisition)

log_cm
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 21  5
##          1 11 62
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.8384          
##                  95% CI : (0.7509, 0.9047)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.0002193       
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.6116          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.2112995       
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.6562          
##             Specificity : 0.9254          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.8077          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.8493          
##              Prevalence : 0.3232          
##          Detection Rate : 0.2121          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.2626          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.7908          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : 0               
##

Decison Tree

set.seed(123)

tree_real <- rpart(
  acquisition ~ .,
  data = train_real,
  method = "class")

rpart.plot(tree_real)

pred_real <- predict(tree_real, test_real, type = "class")

tree_cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_real, test_real$acquisition)

tree_cm
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 15 10
##          1 17 57
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.7273         
##                  95% CI : (0.6285, 0.812)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768         
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.1670         
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.3389         
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.2482         
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.4688         
##             Specificity : 0.8507         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.6000         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.7703         
##              Prevalence : 0.3232         
##          Detection Rate : 0.1515         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.2525         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.6597         
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : 0              
##

Random Forest

set.seed(123)

rf_model <- train(
  acquisition ~ .,
  data = train_real,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10),
  tuneLength = 5)
## note: only 2 unique complexity parameters in default grid. Truncating the grid to 2 .
rf_model
## Random Forest 
## 
## 401 samples
##   3 predictor
##   2 classes: '0', '1' 
## 
## No pre-processing
## Resampling: Cross-Validated (10 fold) 
## Summary of sample sizes: 361, 361, 361, 361, 361, 360, ... 
## Resampling results across tuning parameters:
## 
##   mtry  Accuracy   Kappa    
##   2     0.7354878  0.3828644
##   3     0.7304878  0.3750685
## 
## Accuracy was used to select the optimal model using the largest value.
## The final value used for the model was mtry = 2.

Best mtry = 2 Accuracy = 0.735 Kappa = 0.383

rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_real)

rf_cm <- confusionMatrix(rf_pred, test_real$acquisition)

rf_cm
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  0  1
##          0 20 12
##          1 12 55
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.7576          
##                  95% CI : (0.6611, 0.8381)
##     No Information Rate : 0.6768          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.05093         
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.4459          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 1.00000         
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.6250          
##             Specificity : 0.8209          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.6250          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.8209          
##              Prevalence : 0.3232          
##          Detection Rate : 0.2020          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3232          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.7229          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : 0               
## 
rf_importance <- varImp(rf_model)

rf_importance
## rf variable importance
## 
##           Overall
## employees  100.00
## revenue     58.53
## industry     0.00
plot(rf_importance, top = 10,
     main = "Random Forest Variable Importance")

Model Comparison

comparison <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Logistic Regression", "Decision Tree", "Random Forest"),
  Accuracy = c(
    log_cm$overall["Accuracy"],
    tree_cm$overall["Accuracy"],
    rf_cm$overall["Accuracy"]),
  Sensitivity = c(
    log_cm$byClass["Sensitivity"],
    tree_cm$byClass["Sensitivity"],
    rf_cm$byClass["Sensitivity"]),
  Specificity = c(
    log_cm$byClass["Specificity"],
    tree_cm$byClass["Specificity"],
    rf_cm$byClass["Specificity"]))

comparison
##                 Model  Accuracy Sensitivity Specificity
## 1 Logistic Regression 0.8383838     0.65625   0.9253731
## 2       Decision Tree 0.7272727     0.46875   0.8507463
## 3       Random Forest 0.7575758     0.62500   0.8208955
